task_completion.description: Load the countries shapefile into gvSIG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' is visible in the gvSIG Table of Contents (left panel) and the countries are rendered on the map.

task_completion.description: Load the populated places shapefile into gvSIG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_populated_places' is visible in the gvSIG Table of Contents and the points are rendered on the map.

task_completion.description: Configure a spatial selection to find isolated territories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Selection by spatial query' (or similar) dialog is open, showing the countries layer as the target/active layer and the populated places as the reference layer, with a disjoint or 'does not intersect' condition.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct countries are selected on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A subset of countries is highlighted (usually in yellow) on the map. Crucially, none of the forbidden countries (United States of America, China, Brazil, India, Russia, Australia, Canada) should be highlighted.

task_completion.description: Export the selected features to the specified shapefile path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog is visible showing the exact path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/isolated_territories.shp', OR a success message is displayed, OR the new 'isolated_territories' layer appears in the Table of Contents.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG Desktop application to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: All spatial operations and exports are performed through the gvSIG graphical user interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use command-line GIS tools to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open where tools like ogr2ogr, GDAL, or Python scripts are being used to perform the spatial selection or export.

integrity.description: The agent used spatial query tools rather than manually clicking to select countries.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using spatial selection dialogs rather than the manual selection pointer tool to click on individual countries one by one.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is /home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/isolated_territories.shp. The correct spatial selection will result in exactly 15 countries being selected. The following forbidden countries contain populated places and MUST NOT be selected: United States of America, China, Brazil, India, Russia, Australia, Canada.
